summary: Iteration 1 — full backend + frontend smoke test of Sankalp Marketing Hub. Backend 17/17 pytest cases pass: /api/health, /api/dashboard (graceful no-schema), 6 list endpoints returning [] when tables missing, POST /api/posts (4xx accepted), AI /api/ai/generate caption + review_reply both return non-empty Claude Sonnet 4.5 output, Google OAuth start redirects to accounts.google.com, Facebook mock redirect to ?code=demo_code_123, disconnect, publish. Frontend: login with admin@sankalp.local works; dashboard renders 4 KPI cards + chart area + upcoming + quick actions + sidebar; all 11 sidebar nav items clickable; Composer overlay (overlay/form/preview/text-en/ai-caption) renders fully; AI caption button generated a 730-char Claude response in EN; Cmd/Ctrl+K opens AI Command overlay, prompt returns a 1638-char structured plan; Integrations page renders 8 platform cards with Connect buttons; Reviews and Blogs views render empty-state correctly. No console errors, no broken images, no React render crashes.

integration_issues.flow: AI Command modal -> Escape -> click nav-integrations
integration_issues.issue: Intermittently observed once: after submitting an AI Command prompt, pressing Escape to close the overlay, then clicking nav-integrations, the sidebar highlighted Integrations but the Dashboard content remained and integration cards (testid integration-card-*) did not render (0 found). Not reproducible on a clean re-run (Ctrl+K -> Escape -> nav-integrations rendered all 8 cards). Likely a transient state from a streaming AI response not being aborted on Escape. Worth verifying that AICommand cancels in-flight fetch on close and that route switching forces a re-render of the main view.

issues: The email field placeholder shows 'you@sankalpinterior.com' but the visible text appears slightly clipped/overlapping with the leading icon at viewport 1920x1080. Cosmetic only.

action_items: (Optional) In AICommand.tsx: abort in-flight fetch on overlay close (AbortController) so a Ctrl+K -> Enter -> Escape sequence cleanly resets and does not block subsequent route changes.
(Optional) In App.tsx route renderer: ensure the active view is keyed by viewId so React unmounts/remounts on nav-* switches and prevents any stale main-content from persisting.
User-side: run /app/supabase_schema.sql in Supabase SQL editor to enable CRUD persistence (already surfaced via friendly dashboard banner).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses os.environ['SUPABASE_URL'] without graceful fallback — correct per spec (fail fast on missing env). Good.
sb_select() correctly swallows PGRST205 / 'does not exist' / 'schema cache' errors and returns []. Good defensive layer.
CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False is fine because no cookies are used. Acceptable.
AI endpoint uses emergentintegrations Claude Sonnet 4.5 correctly and returns {text, task, platform, language}. Latency observed ~6-12s per call — acceptable.
Google OAuth redirect_uri derived from x-forwarded-host header — correct for Kubernetes ingress.
make_crud factory exposes POST without auth — for V1 internal tool this is fine; document that the backend assumes the frontend Supabase Auth has already gated access.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows pass; one intermittent integrations-after-AICommand state observed once, not reproducible on retry)

seed_data_creation: None. Supabase tables are intentionally absent per user instruction; all list endpoints return [] gracefully.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py covers all critical backend paths and runs in ~17s. Frontend flows verified: Login -> Dashboard -> Sidebar nav (11 items) -> Compose overlay -> AI caption -> Ctrl+K AI Command -> Integrations cards (8) -> Reviews/Blogs empty-state. AI calls take 6-15s; allow long timeouts. The user has NOT run supabase_schema.sql yet, so anything that requires actual rows (posts/blogs/reviews lists with data, publish on a real id) is expected to be empty/4xx — this is by design.
rca of the issue: Single intermittent UI quirk: after AI Command modal Escape, one route switch to Integrations failed to re-render main content. Hypothesis: AICommand's streaming/awaiting fetch is not aborted on close, leaving a microtask that briefly blocks the React reconciliation when the route changes. Mitigation: wrap the fetch in an AbortController stored on the overlay's state and call abort() in the onClose/Escape handler; additionally key the main view container in App.tsx by current viewId so any stale tree is unmounted.
